summary.en: A global, mentorship-driven pre-seed accelerator and venture firm that runs three-month programs across many cities and industry verticals worldwide.
summary.zh: 一家全球性、以導師制為核心的種子前(pre-seed)加速器暨創投公司,在世界各地多個城市與產業垂直領域舉辦為期三個月的加速計畫。

model.en: As of 2025, Techstars invests $220,000 per company: $200,000 via an uncapped MFN SAFE plus $20,000 via a post-money convertible equity agreement, for a minimum 5% stake plus whatever the uncapped SAFE later converts into. Programs run roughly three months and are heavily mentor-driven, organized by city and vertical.
model.zh: 自 2025 年起,Techstars 每家公司投資 22 萬美元:其中 20 萬美元透過無上限 MFN SAFE、另外 2 萬美元透過 post-money 可轉換股權協議,換取至少 5% 股權,外加無上限 SAFE 日後轉換的部分。計畫約為期三個月,高度仰賴導師指導,並依城市與產業別分別舉辦。

vs_yc.en: Unlike YC's single large batch run mostly from one hub, Techstars is decentralized into many small, city- and theme-specific programs each led by local managing directors, and its model leans more on intensive 1:1 mentorship than on YC's peer-network and demo-day scale.
vs_yc.zh: 不同於 YC 主要集中在單一據點、規模龐大的單一梯隊,Techstars 將計畫拆分為許多小型、依城市與主題劃分的方案,各由當地的董事總經理(Managing Director)領軍;其模式更倚重密集的一對一導師輔導,而非 YC 那種同儕網絡與 Demo Day 的規模效應。

sources.title: Techstars increases startup funding to $220,000, mirroring YC structure (TechCrunch)

summary.en: An early-stage venture fund and seed accelerator known for its global reach, founded in 2010 by Dave McClure and Christine Tsai and rebranded from 500 Startups to 500 Global in 2021.
summary.zh: 一家以全球觸角著稱的早期創投基金暨種子加速器,2010 年由 Dave McClure 與 Christine Tsai 創立,並於 2021 年從「500 Startups」更名為「500 Global」。

model.en: Its Flagship Accelerator offers $150,000 for a 6% stake, with a $37,500 program fee deducted so companies net about $112,500. The flagship program is an in-person batch run in Silicon Valley; the firm is one of the most active early-stage investors globally, having backed thousands of companies across 80+ countries.
model.zh: 其旗艦加速器(Flagship Accelerator)提供 15 萬美元換 6% 股權,並扣除 3.75 萬美元的計畫費用,公司實拿約 11.25 萬美元。旗艦計畫以實體梯隊形式在矽谷舉辦;該公司是全球最活躍的早期投資者之一,已投資橫跨 80 多國的數千家公司。

vs_yc.en: 500 Global charges an explicit program fee and takes a different equity split, and it positions itself as a globally distributed VC plus accelerator with heavy emerging-markets exposure, rather than YC's US-centric, fee-free standard deal and dominant Silicon Valley brand.
vs_yc.zh: 500 Global 會明確收取計畫費用、採用不同的股權拆分方式,並把自己定位為一家全球佈局的創投加上加速器、對新興市場曝險甚高;相較之下,YC 採取以美國為核心、不收費的標準條件,並擁有主導性的矽谷品牌。

summary.en: An early-stage 'day zero' venture firm founded in 2017 by Magnus Grimeland and Fridtjof Berge that helps individuals find co-founders and build companies from scratch, operating across more than 30 cities globally.
summary.zh: 一家「第零天(day zero)」的早期創投公司,2017 年由 Magnus Grimeland 與 Fridtjof Berge 創立,協助個人媒合共同創辦人並從零打造公司,業務遍及全球 30 多個城市。

model.en: Antler runs a residency where individuals join without an idea or team, form co-founding partnerships, and then receive investment in the startups that emerge—typically an initial $150,000 (often structured as $100,000 for ~10% via SAFE plus $50,000 via an uncapped MFN SAFE), with substantial follow-on matching capital available.
model.zh: Antler 舉辦一種「駐點(residency)」計畫,參與者可在尚無點子或團隊的情況下加入,於計畫中組成共同創辦團隊,再由 Antler 投資因此誕生的新創——通常是初始 15 萬美元(常拆分為 10 萬美元換約 10% 股權的 SAFE,加上 5 萬美元的無上限 MFN SAFE),並提供可觀的後續配比資金。

vs_yc.en: Antler enters earlier than YC: it backs people before they even have a co-founder or company, running a structured co-founder matching and company-formation residency, whereas YC funds already-formed teams with a product or idea.
vs_yc.zh: Antler 切入的時點比 YC 更早:它在創辦人連共同創辦人或公司都還沒有時就投資「人」,並提供一套結構化的共同創辦人媒合與公司成形駐點;相較之下,YC 投資的是已經組好團隊、已有產品或點子的新創。

summary.en: A small, highly selective seed accelerator launched in September 2010 by ex-Google product manager Thomas Korte and Carine Magescas, operating out of San Francisco and New York.
summary.zh: 一家規模精小、極度挑剔的種子加速器,2010 年 9 月由前 Google 產品經理 Thomas Korte 與 Carine Magescas 創立,據點設於舊金山與紐約。

model.en: AngelPad runs two ten-week cohorts per year, picking only roughly 15–20 teams from about 4,000 applicants each cycle. It has invested on the order of $100,000–$120,000 per startup and is run hands-on by a tiny partner team rather than a large staff.
model.zh: AngelPad 每年舉辦兩個為期十週的梯隊,每屆約從 4,000 件申請中只挑選大約 15 至 20 個團隊。它對每家新創投資約 10 萬至 12 萬美元,並由極小的合夥人團隊親力親為地經營,而非龐大的工作人員編制。

vs_yc.en: AngelPad is deliberately tiny and boutique—very small classes with intensive, partner-led attention—in direct contrast to YC's massive, scaled batches of hundreds of companies and large alumni network.
vs_yc.zh: AngelPad 刻意保持精品化的小規模——班級極小、由合夥人親自高度投入——與 YC 動輒數百家公司的大規模梯隊及龐大校友網絡形成鮮明對比。

summary.en: A fully remote, globally accessible 'startup generator' founded in 2018 by Daniel Gross and Rishi Narang, designed to find and back ambitious 'outsiders' anywhere in the world.
summary.zh: 一家完全遠端、面向全球的「新創孵化器(startup generator)」,2018 年由 Daniel Gross 與 Rishi Narang 創立,旨在發掘並支持世界各地有野心的「局外人(outsiders)」。

model.en: Pioneer ran a gamified monthly online 'tournament' in which applicants earned points by making weekly progress; winners became Pioneers and received a small initial investment (around $5,000) with the possibility of a $100,000 follow-on, mentorship, and access to a private community. It stopped making new investments in 2024.
model.zh: Pioneer 經營一種遊戲化的每月線上「錦標賽(tournament)」,申請者透過每週推進進度來累積積分;勝出者成為「Pioneer」,可獲得一筆小額初始投資(約 5,000 美元),並有機會取得 10 萬美元後續投資、導師輔導與一個私密社群的存取權。該公司已於 2024 年停止進行新投資。

vs_yc.en: Pioneer was 100% remote and tournament-based, using a public leaderboard and gamified weekly progress to discover talent globally—an explicit contrast to YC's in-person batch, larger checks, and demo-day-centric model.
vs_yc.zh: Pioneer 完全遠端、以錦標賽為機制,透過公開排行榜與遊戲化的每週進度在全球範圍發掘人才——與 YC 的實體梯隊、較大金額的投資以及以 Demo Day 為核心的模式形成明顯對照。

sources.title: Pioneer founder Daniel Gross on bringing remote teams together (TechCrunch)

summary.en: A 'talent investor' founded in 2011 in London by Matt Clifford and Alice Bentinck that backs exceptional individuals before they have a company or co-founder, helping them form teams and build startups.
summary.zh: 一家「人才投資者(talent investor)」,2011 年由 Matt Clifford 與 Alice Bentinck 在倫敦創立,在創辦人尚無公司或共同創辦人之前就投資傑出的個人,協助他們組隊並打造新創。

model.en: Participants join an intensive ~12-week program (FORM) as individuals (Founders-in-Residence), get matched into co-founding pairs, and develop ideas. EF's pre-seed offer includes a roughly $125,000 post-money SAFE for about 8%, with an additional optional uncapped MFN SAFE, plus an upfront talent stipend during the program.
model.zh: 參與者以個人身分(駐點創辦人,Founders-in-Residence)加入一個密集約 12 週的計畫(FORM),媒合成共同創辦的搭檔,並發展點子。EF 的種子前投資條件包含約 12.5 萬美元、換約 8% 股權的 post-money SAFE,外加一筆可選的無上限 MFN SAFE,並在計畫期間提供一筆預付的人才津貼。

vs_yc.en: Like Antler, EF invests at the pre-team stage and specializes in co-founder matching and company formation, whereas YC requires an existing team and idea; EF is also rooted in Europe and Asia rather than Silicon Valley.
vs_yc.zh: 與 Antler 類似,EF 在團隊尚未成形的階段就投資,專精於共同創辦人媒合與公司成形;而 YC 要求已有團隊與點子。此外,EF 的根基在歐洲與亞洲,而非矽谷。

sources.title: Updating our global pre-seed investment offer (official EF)

summary.en: HAX is the hard-tech / deep-tech pre-seed program operated by the venture fund SOSV, founded in 2011 by Cyril Ebersweiler (with SOSV's Sean O'Sullivan), with its first program run in Shenzhen in 2012.
summary.zh: HAX 是由創投基金 SOSV 營運的硬科技(hard tech)/深科技(deep tech)種子前計畫,2011 年由 Cyril Ebersweiler(與 SOSV 的 Sean O'Sullivan)創立,首屆計畫於 2012 年在深圳舉辦。

model.en: HAX provides up to about $550,000 in pre-seed funding (an initial $250,000–$500,000 from SOSV) and requires founders to relocate for a roughly six-month, lab-based residency. Its Newark facility houses machine shops and mechanical, chemical, and electronics labs to support physical-product founders in climate, industrial automation, and health.
model.zh: HAX 提供高達約 55 萬美元的種子前資金(來自 SOSV 的初始 25 萬至 50 萬美元),並要求創辦人搬遷到當地、進行約六個月、以實驗室為基地的駐點。其位於紐瓦克(Newark)的場地設有機械工坊以及機械、化學與電子實驗室,支援氣候、工業自動化與健康領域的實體產品創辦人。

vs_yc.en: HAX is a specialist deep-tech / hardware accelerator with hands-on lab infrastructure and larger checks tied to a multi-month in-residence build, versus YC's sector-agnostic, software-leaning model with a smaller standard check and no physical lab.
vs_yc.zh: HAX 是專注於深科技/硬體的加速器,具備可動手操作的實驗室基礎設施,並提供與多月實體駐點開發綁定的較大金額投資;相較之下,YC 採取不限產業、偏向軟體的模式,標準投資金額較小且不提供實體實驗室。

summary.en: A company-building immersion program launched by top-tier VC Sequoia Capital in 2022, run twice a year for small cohorts of pre-seed and seed-stage founders in the Americas and Europe.
summary.zh: 由頂級創投紅杉資本(Sequoia Capital)於 2022 年推出的「公司打造沉浸式計畫(company-building immersion)」,每年舉辦兩次,面向美洲與歐洲種子前及種子階段的小型創辦人梯隊。

model.en: Every company accepted into Arc receives a $1 million upfront investment from Sequoia, made immediately on acceptance and before the program begins. Terms are company-specific rather than standardized; Sequoia does not publish a fixed equity percentage. Cohorts are kept to roughly 10 companies, centered on a multi-day 'Arc Intensive' curriculum.
model.zh: 每家進入 Arc 的公司都會在獲選時、計畫開始前立即收到紅杉的 100 萬美元預付投資。其條件是依公司個別議定,而非標準化;紅杉並未公布固定的股權比例。梯隊規模維持在約 10 家公司,核心是一套為期數日的「Arc Intensive」課程。

vs_yc.en: Arc is a big-VC seed program with a much larger upfront check, bespoke (non-standardized) terms, and a tiny hand-picked cohort—the opposite of YC's standardized take-it-or-leave-it deal and large-scale batch.
vs_yc.zh: Arc 是由大型創投主導的種子計畫,提供大得多的預付支票、量身議定(非標準化)的條件,以及精挑細選的極小梯隊——與 YC 那種標準化、要不要隨你的條件以及大規模梯隊正好相反。

sources.title: Accelerators see a spike in interest as VCs like Sequoia and Accel look to bulk up seed stage investing (Fortune via Yahoo)

summary.en: A Silicon Valley innovation platform and venture firm founded in 2006 by Saeed Amidi that connects startups with Fortune 500 corporations and investors across many industry verticals.
summary.zh: 一個由 Saeed Amidi 於 2006 年創立的矽谷創新平台暨創投公司,串連新創與《財星》500 大企業及投資者,橫跨眾多產業垂直領域。

model.en: Plug and Play runs many industry-specific accelerator programs (e.g., fintech, mobility, health, insurtech, sustainability), most of them non-equity, that pair startups with corporate partners for pilots, proofs-of-concept, and potential investment or acquisition; it has been among the most active startup investors in the world by deal count.
model.zh: Plug and Play 經營許多依產業劃分的加速計畫(例如金融科技、移動出行、健康、保險科技、永續),其中多數不收取股權,讓新創與企業夥伴在試點(pilot)、概念驗證(PoC)、潛在投資或收購上對接;就交易筆數而言,它是全球最活躍的新創投資者之一。

vs_yc.en: Plug and Play is built around corporate innovation and matchmaking—mostly non-equity, vertically themed programs that broker startup-corporate pilots—rather than YC's equity-for-cash standard deal, peer batch, and demo-day-to-VC pipeline.
vs_yc.zh: Plug and Play 圍繞企業創新與媒合而建——多為不收股權、依垂直主題設計的計畫,撮合新創與企業的試點合作——而非 YC 那種以股權換現金的標準條件、同儕梯隊,以及從 Demo Day 通往創投的管道。

sources.title: Plug and Play Tech Center - Wikipedia
Plug and Play Tech Center official site
